messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show ip aspath-list command do?

The show ip aspath-list command shows the configuration details of the AS path list. Specifies name of the AS path list. <NAME> vsx-peer Shows the configuration details of the community-list. Specifies name of the community-list. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. <NAME> vsx-peer Shows the configuration details of the IP prefix lists. Specifies name of the IP prefix lists. <NAME> vsx-peer Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show ip aspath-list [<NAME>] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities smartlink command do?

The show capacities smartlink command shows Smartlink capacities or Smartlink capacities and status. Enables the notification of events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations. It is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the event traps. Public event-trap-enable 3957 Enables sending SNMP traps for LLDP related events from a particular interface. LLDP trap generation is enabled by default on all the interfaces and has to be disabled for interfaces on which traps are not required to be generated. Public lldp trap enable 3958 The no form of this command disables the LLDP trap generation. NOTE LLDP trap generation is disabled by default at the global level and must be enabled before any LLDP traps are sent. Syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink.

syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink

The show mac-notify command displays whether the MAC notification feature in the SNMP module is enabled or not. It also displays the trap notification types configured on the Layer 2 ports in the system. Syntax: show mac-notify 3969.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show rmon alarm command do?

The show rmon alarm command displays the RMON alarm configurations. Specifies the RMON alarm index. Range: 1 to 20. Displays SNMP agent UDP port number. Displays a list of all configured SNMP v1/v2c communities. Displays all configured SNMP contexts. Displays the configured SNMP v3 snmp engine-id. If the SNMP v3 engine-id is not configured, by default a unique engine-id is created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Displays the configured SNMP v3 security level. Displays SNMP description, location, and contact information. Displays all configured SNMP traps/informs receivers. Displays all configured SNMP v3 users. For more details on the user enabled status, see snmpv3 security-level. Displays the list of all the configured SNMP views. Syntax: show rmon alarm [index <INDEX>].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> [view <VIEW-NAME>] command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> [view <VIEW-NAME>] command associates an SNMP MIB view with the SNMP community. The no form of this command removes the associated SNMP MIB view from the SNMP community. Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Specifies the view name for the SNMP MIB view. Accepts a maxi mum of 32 characters. <STRING> <VIEW-NAME>. Syntax: no snmp-server community <STRING> [view <VIEW-NAME>].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. <STRING> Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: no snmp-server community <STRING>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server snmpv3-only command do?

The snmp-server snmpv3-only command accepts SNMP v3 messages only, SNMP v1 and SNMP v2c will be disabled. By default SNMP v1, SNMP v2c and SNMP v3 will all be enabled. The no form of this command restores the default setting and reenables SNMP v1 and SNMP v2c. Syntax:
- no snmp-server snmpv3-only
- snmp-server snmpv3-only 4015.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status command do?

The snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status command enables the SNMP trap for AAA server status. When enabled, traps are sent whenever AAA server (RADIUS, TACACS) status changes from reachable to unreachable and vice versa. The no form of this command disables sending SNMP trap for AAA server status. Syntax:
- no snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status
- snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status 4016.

syntax:
- no snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status
- snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status 4016

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server trap configuration-changes command do?

The snmp-server trap configuration-changes command enables sending SNMP traps whenever the configuration changes. Configuration trap generation is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables sending SNMP traps for configuration changes. configuration-changes Specifies SNMP traps for configuration changes. Syntax:
- no snmp-server trap configuration-changes
- snmp-server trap configuration-changes 4017.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the snmp-server view <VIEWNAME> command do?

The snmp-server view <VIEWNAME> command configures an SNMP MIB view. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP MIB view. Specifies the name of the SNMP MIB view. Supports up to a ma ximum of 32 characters. Specifies the OID tree to be included or excluded in SNMP MIB view. Specifies the OID mask value. The values must be in hexadecim al character separated with: (colon). Specifies the OID tree that is included in or excluded from the S NMP MIB view. <VIEWNAME> <OID_TREE> <MASK> <included/excluded>. Syntax: no snmp-server view <VIEWNAME>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ssh maximum-auth-attempts command do?

The ssh maximum-auth-attempts command sets the SSH maximum number of authentication attempts. The no form of the command resets the maximum to its default of 6. Specifies the maximum number of SSH authentication attempts. Range: 1 to 10. Default: 6. <ATTEMPTS> Configures SSH to use a set of public key algorithms in the specified priority order. The first public key type entered in the CLI is considered a first priority. Public key algorithms specify which public key types can be used for public key authentication in SSH. Each option represents a public key type that the SSH server can accept or that the SSH client can present to a server. Only the public key algorithms that are chosen by the user are configured. Syntax: ssh maximum-auth-attempts <ATTEMPTS>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities-status command do?

The show capacities-status command shows system capacities status and their values for all features or a specific feature. <FEATURE> vsx-peer Specifies the feature, for example aaa or vrrp for which to displ ay capacities, values, and status. Required. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Shows the serial console port current speed. Shows core dump information about the specified module. When no parameters are specified, shows only the core dumps generated in the current boot of the management module. When the all parameter is specified, shows all available core dumps. Shows all available core dumps. Syntax: show capacities-status <FEATURE>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command do?

The ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command enables SSH enforcement that the username must be present in the certificate that is being used for authorization. This configuration alters how certificate-based authentication maps to a user account. When this is enabled, SSH will not require local user association with an authorized-key and instead enforces that the username used to log in is present within the certificate. The SSH server will check for the username in certificate fields Common Name or User Principle Name for a match. If a certificate is not used for authentication then this configuration has no effect on SSH authentication. The no form of this command disables the SSH enforcement of username in the certificate. Syntax: no ssh certificate-as-authorized-key.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. NOTE VLANs used for internal purposes using the command system internal vlan range cannot be used for any other (L2) purposes. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to 4040. Public vlan (interface) 4436 Creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. Syntax: vlan access <VLAN-ID>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vlan protocol <PROTOCOL_NAME> command do?

The vlan protocol <PROTOCOL_NAME> command adds protocol mapping to a VLAN on an interface. The no form of this command removes protocol mapping from the VLAN on an interface. Specifies a VLAN ID. Range: 2 to 4094. <VLAN-ID> <PROTOCOL_NAME>. Syntax: no vlan protocol <PROTOCOL_NAME>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show active-gateway <IFNAME> command do?

The show active-gateway <IFNAME> command displays the gateway information per SVI, such as: • Active-Gateway IPv4 and its MAC address • Active-Gateway IPv6 and its MAC address Specifies the VSX interface name. <IFNAME> vsx-peer Displays a virtual IPv4/IPv6 and MAC configured for active-active routing. Specifies the VLAN name. Syntax: string <VLAN-NAME> vsx-peer Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Public show interface <VLAN-NAME> 4540 Displays a specified LAG or all configured LAGs along with VSX LAGs. Specifies the LAG name. Optional. Syntax: string <LAG-NAME> vsx-peer Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. Syntax: show active-gateway <IFNAME>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx shutdown-on-split command do?

The vsx shutdown-on-split command shuts down the configured non-VSX interfaces on the VSX secondary along with VSX interfaces during a VSX split. The no form of this command resumes the non-VSX interfaces that are shut down during the VSX split. NOTE This command has no effect on the VSX primary during a split. However, when applied on the VSX primary, the command will bring down the non-VSX interfaces until linkup delay timer expires during the VSX primary reboot. Syntax: no vsx shutdown-on-split.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command do?

The vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command enables VSX sync of active gateways or policies associated under an interface. To synchronize the associations, you must configure the same interface vlan on the peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization for active gateways or policies associated under an interface, but it does not remove the feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. {[active-gateways] [policies]} access-gateways policies. Syntax: no vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]}.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync bfd-global command do?

The vsx-sync bfd-global command enables syncing of global BFD configurations, such as echo-src-ip-address, detect-multip lier, min-transmit-interval, and min-receive-interval, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. NOTE This command enables VSX synchronization only at the top level and not at the context level. The no form of this command disables the syncing of global BFD configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the existing global BFD feature configurations from it. Syntax: no vsx-sync bfd-global.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync dhcp-server command do?

The vsx-sync dhcp-server command enables VSX synchronization of all DHCPv4 server configurations, including external storage configurations, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. Only the primary VSX node answers DHCP service requests, and leases can only be exported from the primary VSX node. The no form of the command disables VSX synchronization of DHCPv4 server configurations to the secondary peer; however, it does not remove the existing DHCPv4 server feature configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync dhcp-server.

To synchronize DHCP snooping configurations associated with a particular VLAN and interface, configure the same VLAN and interface on the peer device. The no form of this command disables syncing DHCP snooping configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync dhcp-snooping.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync evpn command do?

The vsx-sync evpn command enables syncing of all EVPN context-related configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. The no form of this command disables syncing EVPN configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE As a prerequisite, VLAN vsx-sync must be enabled separately for the VLAN configurations inside EVPN context to get synced. Syntax: no vsx-sync evpn.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync keychain command do?

The vsx-sync keychain command enables synchronizing of key chain configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. There is no configuration synchronization from secondary to primary peer. If any additional modification or configuration is made on the primary for the key chain set of features, the features will be auto-synchronized. The no form of the command disables synchronizing key chain configurations to the secondary peer. But it does not remove the previously synchronized configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax:
- no vsx-sync keychain
- vsx-sync keychain 4619.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync loop-protect-global command do?

The vsx-sync loop-protect-global command enables the VSX synchronization of global loop protect configurations, such as transmit-interval and re-enable-timer, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To enable VSX synchronization at the context level for this feature, enter the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command at the context level. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global loop protect configurations, but it does not remove the existing global loop protect feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync loop-protect-global.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command do?

The vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command enables the VSX synchronization of VSX LAG interface associations and attributes on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. The Usage section in this topic provides a listing of specific associations and attributes that are synchronized to the secondary switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global VSX LAG and attributes, but it does not remove the existing VSX LAG feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync mclag-interfaces.

The vsx-sync nd-snooping command enables VSX synchronization of ND snooping configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize ND snooping configurations associated with a particular VLAN and interface, configure the same VLAN and interface on the peer device. When RA guard policy is enabled, this command also synchronizes RA guard policy related configurations. The no form of this command disables syncing ND snooping configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync nd-snooping.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync neighbor command do?

The vsx-sync neighbor command enables VSX synchronization of IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configuration on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. If any new modification or additional configuration is made on the primary node for IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configuration, they will be auto-synced. Public vsx-sync neighbor 4626 The no form of this command VSX synchronization of IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync neighbor.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync ospf command do?

The vsx-sync ospf command enables syncing of OSPF (including OSPFv2 and OPSFv3), route map, and key chain configurations on the primary VSX switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize OSPF configurations at the port level context, configure the same port on the peer device. The no form of this command disables syncing of OSPF, route map, and key chain configurations to the secondary peer. But it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE The OSPF router ID is not synchronized. This exclusion is needed because the router ID uniquely identifies the router. The two OSPF routers with the same router ID do not form an adjacency between them. Syntax: no vsx-sync ospf.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync private-vlan-global command do?

Private VLAN global configuration syncing is supported on the switch by configuring the feature name under vsx configuration context. There are no parameters in this command. The no form of this command disables Private VLAN global configuration syncing to the secondary peer. Public vsx-sync private-vlan-global 4632. Syntax: no vsx-sync private-vlan-global.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync route-map command do?

The vsx-sync route-map command enables syncing of all As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from the secondary to primary peer. The no form of this command disables syncing of As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE When vsx-sync for BGP or OSPF is configured, route map configurations are synchronized from primary VSX node to the secondary peer due to the dependency in configurations. Syntax: no vsx-sync route-map.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync stp-global command do?

The vsx-sync stp-global command enables the VSX synchronization of global STP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. Use the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command to sync context level spanning trees. To enable VSX synchronization at the context level for this feature, enter the vsx-sync mclag-inte rfaces command at the context level. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global STP configurations, but it does not remove the existing global STP feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync stp-global.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync telnet command do?

The vsx-sync telnet command enables VSX synchronization of Telnet server configurations from the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. To synchronize Telnet configurations associated with a particular VRF, you need to configure the same VRF on the peer device. The no form of the command disables VSX synchronization of Telnet server configurations to the secondary peer, however, it does not remove the existing Telnet server configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync telnet.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vsx-sync vrrp command do?

The vsx-sync vrrp command enables VSX synchronization of all VRRP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize VRRP configurations at the port level context, the same port must be configured on the peer device with IP address. Public vsx-sync vrrp 4647 The no form of this command disables syncing VRRP configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. NOTE BFD IP is the IP address of VRRP peer device. Hence it cannot be synced. In the owner scenario, in case the priority is synced, both VSX primary and secondary devices will have 255 as their priority. Syntax: no vsx-sync vrrp.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the erase all zeroize command do?

The erase all zeroize command restores the switch to its factory default configuration. You will be prompted before the procedure starts. Once complete, the switch will restart from the primary image with factory default settings. Syntax: erase [all] zeroize.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show udld command do?

The show udld command displays UDLD information for all interfaces or for a specific interface. interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Specifies the name of a logical interface on the switch, which ca n be: • An Ethernet interface associated with a physical port. Use t he format member/slot/port (for example, 1/3/1). • UDLD runs only on physical interfaces. LAGs, tunnels, and t he like are not supported. However, UDLD can be configure d individually on each port of a LAG or trunk group. Config uring UDLD on a trunk group primary port enables UDLD o n that port only. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show udld [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip forward-protocol udp command do?

The ip forward-protocol udp command defines the UDP server to which the interface forwards ingress UDP broadcast packets received on a specific UDP port. A maximum of 8 UDP broadcast servers can be configured per interface. The no form of this command removes traffic forwarding for the specified server and port/protocol. <IPv4-ADDR> <PORT-NUM> <PROTOCOL> Specifies the UDP server IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), wh ere x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Specifies the UDP port number for which traffic is forwarded. Specifies the protocol name for which traffic is forwarded. Syntax: ip forward-protocol udp <IPv4-ADDR> {<PORT-NUM> | <PROTOCOL>}.

Syntax: show capacities-status vlan-translation.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show vlan voice command do?

The show vlan voice command displays the voice VLAN list showing the VLAN ID, name, operational state of the VLAN, and the interfaces associated with the VLAN. Disables a VLAN. (By default, a VLAN is automatically enabled when it is created with the vlan command.) The no form of this command enables a VLAN. Indicates if dynamically learned VLANs from MVRP and port-access should be included or excluded on ports configured with vlan trunk allowed all. By default, dynamic VLANs are not included in the trunk allowed list. This command is used at the system-level. The no form of this command disables the inclusion of dynamic VLANs in the VLANs table. This is the default. Enables the Unknown Unicast Flood Block (UUFB) feature on a physical interface. Syntax: show vlan voice.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the vlan (interface) command do?

The vlan (interface) command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. NOTE VLANs used for internal purposes using the command system internal vlan range cannot be used for any other (L2) purposes. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to 4040. Public vlan (interface) 4436. Syntax: vlan <VLAN-LIST>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the router vrrp {enable disable} command do?

The router vrrp {enable disable} command enables or disables VRRP protocol globally. You must globally enable the VRRP feature for VRRP virtual router. no router vrrp enable disables VRRP protocol globally but does not remove all VRRP configurations. no router vrrp disable enables VRRP protocol globally. Syntax: router vrrp {enable | disable}.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the interface lag multi-chassis command do?

The interface lag multi-chassis command configures a given LAG as a dynamic multichassis LAG (VSX LAG), which supports a maximum of four member links per switch segment. A VSX LAG across a downstream switch can have at most a total of eight member links. The no form of this command removes a VSX LAG. Public interface lag multi-chassis 4520 Specifies the LAG ID. Run the show capacities vsx c ommand for the maximum number of VSX LAGs supported for your particular type of switch; however, the maximum VSX LAG value considers that one port is used for the ISL, which is not a VSX LAG. Required. Specifies the multichassis LAG as static. Optional. <LAG-ID> static. Syntax: interface lag <LAG-ID> multi-chassis [static].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip icmp redirect (VSX) command do?

The ip icmp redirect (VSX) command enables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 redirect messages to the source host. Enabled by default. The no form of this command disables ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 redirect messages to the source host. Syntax: ip icmp redirect.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude command do?

The neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude command excludes the neighbor configurations from VSX sync operations at run time. When VSX sync is configured for BGP, the VSX secondary peer cannot have any configuration of its own. If the VSX primary has a configuration, that will always get synchronized to the VSX secondary. Any modification done in the synchronized configurations on the VSX secondary will be overwritten by the VSX primary configuration after a VSX config sync. If there is a new configuration on the VSX secondary, then that configuration also gets deleted after a VSX config sync. On the VSX primary, a configuration with vsx-sync-exclude enabled excludes the neighbor configurations from synchronizing to the VSX -secondary, allowing the VSX secondary can have it own entry for the corresponding neighbor configurations. Syntax: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show active-gateway command do?

The show active-gateway command displays the gateway information configured on SVIs, such as: • Number of active-gateway interface VLANs • Number of IPv4 active-gateway interface VLANs • Number of IPv6 active-gateway interface VLANs • Per virtual MAC address ◦ ◦ IPv4 reference count and its interface VLANs IPv6 reference count and its interface VLANs Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show active-gateway [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show lacp interfaces (vsx) command do?

The show lacp interfaces (vsx) command displays an LACP configuration of the physical interfaces, including VSX s. If an interface name is passed as argument, it only displays an LACP configuration of a specified interface. Optional: Specifies an interface name. <IFNAME> Public show lacp interfaces (vsx) 4543 Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. This example displays an LACP configuration of the physical interfaces. One of the interfaces has the lacp -block forwarding state. If a VSX switch has loop protect enabled on an interface and a loop occurs, VSX blocks the interface to stop the loop. Syntax: show lacp interfaces [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer].

Specifies the datapath for an IPv6 address and its specified sub net. Optional. Syntax: A. B. C. D/M vrf <VRF-NAME> Shows the IPv6 datapath for a specified VRF. vsx-peer. Specifies the route information for an IPv4 address and its specif ied subnet. Optional. Syntax: A. B. C. D/M Specifies routes that are present only on the primary switch o r only on the secondary switch. The routes that are present on both the primary and secondary switch are excluded. Optional. messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show vsx status command do?

The show vsx status command displays global VSX status or a specified status determined by the selected parameter. [inter-switch-link | keepalive | linkup-delay] Selects one of the following: inter-switch-link, kee palive, or linkup-delay inter-switch-link Specifies the display of the ISL status in VSX. keepalive Specifies the display of the VSX keepalive protocol status. linkup-delay vsx-peer Specifies the display of the VSX link-up delay information, suc h as the: • Configured link-up delay timer. • Delay timer status. Initial sync status. • • LAGs on which the delay timer is running. • Status of the LAGs excluded from the link-up delay timer. • • Interfaces that are shut down during VSX split. Interfaces that are shut down during VSX split Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. Syntax: show vsx status [inter-switch-link | keepalive | linkup-delay] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show vsx status peering command do?

The show vsx status peering command displays synchronization peering status and hardware peering status for modules such as MAC, neighbor, spanning-tree, and route. This command can be used to view the status of VSX peering, following a VSX device reboot or an ISL flap. Following are the possible values for VSX peering status: • Complete-VSX peering process is successfully completed. Public show vsx status peering 4581 • In-progress-VSX peering is being processed. • Not-started-VSX peering process is yet to be started. Syntax: show vsx status peering.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the interface vxlan command do?

The interface vxlan command creates VXLAN interface 1 and changes to the config-vxlan-if context. A maximum of one VXLAN interface is supported. By default, the VXLAN is disabled. To enable the VXLAN, use the command no shutdown. The no form of this command removes VXLAN interface 1. This deletes the VXLAN tunnel, and all VNIs and VLAN-to-VNI mappings associated with it. Only one VXLAN interface is supported. (Optional) Specify if the interface tunnel uses IPv4 or IPv6 add ressing. If this parameter is not included, the interface default is IPv6. Syntax: interface vxlan 1 [mode IPv4|IPv6].

The show interface vxlan vni command shows detailed VNI information for the VXLAN interface. Specifies the number for a given VNI. Range: 1 to 16777214. Shows the VTEPS information for a specified VNI. transport-VRFshows the transport VRFstatus for a specified VNI. Syntax: show interface vxlan vni <VNI-ID> [vteps | transport-vrf].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show interface vxlan vteps command do?

The show interface vxlan vteps command displays detailed VTEP information for the VXLAN interface. VTEP states can be one of the following: • operational: Tunnel endpoint is fully programmed on the switch hardware. • configuration_error: Tunnel endpoint programming in the switch hardware failed due to misconfiguration. • no_hw_resources: Tunnel endpoint programming failed in the switch hardware due to insufficient resources. • activating: Initial state of tunnel endpoint when it is configured. When a tunnel endpoint is a directly connected via nexthop, then nexthop reachability appears empty (--). Show detail VTEP information. Specifies the IP address of a VTEP peer in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Syntax: show interface vxlan vteps [detail | <IP-ADDR>].

source_file: 8100/10_18/Quality of Service Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the apply qos threshold-profile command do?

The apply qos threshold-profile command applies a threshold profile globally to all Ethernet and LAG interfaces on the switch, or to a specific interface. When applied globally, the specified threshold profile is configured only on Ethernet interfaces and LAGs that do not already have their own schedule profile. The same profile can be applied both globally and locally to an interface. This guarantees that an interface always uses the specified threshold profile, even if the global profile is changed. The no form of this command removes the specified threshold profile from an interface, and causes it to use the global threshold profile. This is the only way to remove a threshold profile override from an interface. A profile can only be deleted once it is no longer applied to any interface. Syntax: apply qos threshold-profile <THRESHOLD-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear queue-monitor interface command do?

The clear queue-monitor interface command clear the data collected on an interface since enabling queue monitoring. If no interfaces are specified, perform a clear of data collected on all interfaces with queue monitoring enabled. Name of the interface. Format: <MEMBER>/<SLOT>/<PORT> <IFRANGE> Range of interfaces. Format: <MEMBER>/<SLOT>/<PORT>-<MEMBER>/<SLOT> /<PORT> queue-monitor This command enables the queue monitoring feature, allowing the switch to coll ect queue statistics at 10-second time intervals. These aggregate statistics ca n be used to monitor network health, troubleshoot network issues, and identify normal network behavior patterns and performance anomalies. This feature is di sabled by default, but can be enabled on a maximum of 52 interfaces per switch. Syntax: clear queue-monitor interface [<IFNAME>|<IFRANGE>].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos cos command do?

The qos cos command configures a CoS PCP remark for an Ethernet or LAG interface. Packets that ingress on the interface are remarked at egress using the configured CoS PCP value. The remark only occurs when QoS trust mode on the interface is set to none. If QoS trust mode is not set to none, then the remark is ignored, and the following commands will show the CoS remark status as ignored (incompatible Port Access Trust configuration) or not applied' (incompatible QoS global/port Trust configuration): • show running-configuration • show interface <PORT-NUM> • show interface <PORT-NUM> qos The no form of this command removes a CoS remark on an interface. Specifies an 802.1 VLAN priority CoS value. Range: 0 to 7. <CODE-POINT>. Syntax: qos cos <CODE-POINT>.

document_title: Quality Of Service Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series)

source_file: 8100/10_18/Quality of Service Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos dscp command do?

The qos dscp command configures a differentiated services code point (DSCP) remark for an Ethernet or LAG interface. IPv4 and IPv6 packets that ingress on the interface are remarked at egress using the configured DSCP value. The remark only occurs when QoS trust mode on the interface is set to none. If a DSCP remark is configured and then trust mode is subsequently set to cos or dscp, then the DSCP remark is ignored. The following commands will show the remark status as ignored (incompatible Port Access Trust configuration) or not applied (incompatible QoS global or port trust configuration): • show running-configuration • show interface <INTERFACE-NAME> • show interface <INTERFACE-NAME> qos The no form of this command removes a CoS remark on an interface. Syntax: qos dscp <CODE-POINT>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos egress-rewrite command do?

The qos egress-rewrite command enables a rewrite of the 802.1Q VLAN priority code point (PCP) or DSCP values for all traffic egressing on the configured interface. The PCP or DHCP value is determined by a globally configured egress priority rewrite map based on the traffic's local priority. Use this command to configure a system-wide mapping from internal local priorities to 802.1Q VLAN priority code point (PCP) or DSCP values. The no form of this command disables egress priority rewrite on the interface. Public qos egress-rewrite 92 Use show qos egress-rewrite-map to view the current global egress priority rewrite map. Use show qos egress-rewrite-map default to view the factory default egress priority rewrite map. Syntax: qos egress-rewrite [cos|dscp].

The syntax of the qos egress-rewrite command is: qos egress-rewrite [cos|dscp]

document_title: Quality Of Service Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series)
source_file: 8100/10_18/Quality of Service Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos egress-rewrite-map command do?

The qos egress-rewrite-map command configures a system-wide mapping from internal local priority to an 802.1Q VLAN priority code point (PCP) or DSCP value. The qos egress-rewrite cos command allows the PCP value to be rewritten on egress, and the qos egress-rewrite dscp command allows the DSCP value to be rewritten on egress. The local priority associated with a packet affects only its behavior within the switch. By default, it does not influence the priority value written to the packet upon transmission. This command, along with its no form, accepts a single local priority, a range of local priorities, or a comma-separated list of local priorities and ranges. This provides a convenient method for configuring multiple entries in the map. Syntax: qos egress-rewrite-map local-priority <PRIORITY-RANGE> {cos <PCP-VALUE>}.

The syntax of the qos egress-rewrite-map command is: qos egress-rewrite-map local-priority <PRIORITY-RANGE> {cos <PCP-VALUE>}.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos queue-profile command do?

The qos queue-profile command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile. A queue profile maps queues to local-priority values. Each profile has one to eight queues numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS queue profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied can be removed. <NAME> Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). Public qos queue-profile 96. Syntax: qos queue-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos shape command do?

The qos shape command limits the egress bandwidth on an interface to a value that is lower than its line rate. Errors will be generated in the following events: • A user configures a port-shaping value that is greater than 100 percent • A user configures a kbps port-shaping value that is less than the supported minimum kbps value. The supported minimum kbps shaping value can be retrieved using the show capacities command. The no form of this command removes shaping from an interface. Specifies the maximum traffic rate in kbps. Range: 1 to 10000 0000. Alternatively, the bandwidth can also be configured as a percentage of link bandwidth. The supported range is 1-100. Default units are kilobits per second. <RATE>. Syntax: qos shape <RATE> [kbps|percent].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos threshold-profile command do?

The qos threshold-profile command creates a QoS threshold profile and switches to the config-threshold context for the profile. If the specified threshold profile exists, this command switches to the config-threshold context for the existing profile. The threshold profile determines the action to take when a threshold is exceeded for each queue. A threshold profile is composed of up to 7 queues, numbered from 0-6. Each queue defines the action to take when buffer utilization exceeds a specific threshold. Configure queues with the command queue. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS threshold profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied to an interface can be removed. Specifies the name of the QoS threshold profile to create or co nfigure. Syntax: qos threshold-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the qos trust command do?

The qos trust command in the config context: • This command sets the trust mode that is globally applied to all interfaces that do not have a trust mode configured. • The no form of this command restores all interfaces that do not currently have a trust mode configured to the default setting. In the config-if context: • This command sets the trust mode override for a specific interface. • The no form of this command clears a trust mode override. The interface then uses the global setting. This is the only way to remove a trust mode override.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show interface queue-monitor command do?

The show interface queue-monitor command display queue statistics history in the CLI as either a list or a histogram, or as a table, showing the previous five minutes of queue statistics history. interface [<IFNAME>] Specifies the name of an Ethernet port or LAG on the switch. If the optional <IFNAME> parameter is omitted, the output of this command displays all queue statistic data for all interfaces. Format: <MEMBER>/<SLOT>/<PORT> or lag <ID> histogram|list|table Specifies the output format for queue monitor output. • histogram - Queue statistics history is presented in histog • ram format for the last 8 hours. list - Queue statistics history is presented in the list form at for the last 8 hours • table - Queue statistics history is presented in tabular for mat for the last 5 minutes. Syntax: show interface {<IFNAME>} queue-monitor.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show interface queue-monitor status command do?

If no interface is specified, the command will display the queue monitoring status for all interfaces configured. {<IFNAME> | <IFRANGE>} Use the queue-monitor status parameters to show whether t he feature is enabled or disabled, and information about memor Public show interface queue-monitor status 132 y consumption and monitored interfaces. Include the optional <I FNAME> parameter to filter the output of this command to sho w data for just the specified interface, or include both the <IFN AME > parameter and the additional <IFRANGE> parameter als o to view data for a range of interfaces. Syntax: show interface {<IFNAME> | <IFRANGE>} queue-monitor status [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the crypto pki application command do?

The crypto pki application command configures an application to associate with a user-configured certificate. By default, all applications are associated with the default certificate "local-cert". Note that applications "captive-portal" and "hsc" are only available on certain platforms. The certificate associated with EST client is global to all EST profiles. Public PKI commands 405 Among the available applications, only dot1x-supplicant, est-client, and radsec-client are allowed to associate with device-identity certificate. Also note that device-identity certificate on a switch using a TPM of legacy version 1.2 only works with TLS version 1.2. 1. Syntax: crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME>.

source_file: 8100/10_18/Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the crypto pki application command?
The syntax of the crypto pki application command is: crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME>

document_title: Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series)
source_file: 8100/10_18/Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the crypto pki ta-profile command do?

The crypto pki ta-profile command creates a trust anchor (TA) profile and changes to the config-ta-<TA-NAME> context for the profile. Each TA profile stores the certificate for a trusted CA. Up to 64 profiles can be defined. If the specified TA profile exists, this command changes to the config-ta-<TA-NAME> context for the profile. The no form of this command removes the specified TA profile. NOTE When creating a new profile, If you exit the config-ta-<TA-NAME> context without importing the TA certificate, the profile is discarded. <TA-NAME> Specifies the TA profile name. Range: 1 to 48 alphanumeric cha racters excluding ". NOTE The TA profile name cannot end with est-ta<n n> where <nn> is 00 to 99. For example, compa ny-trust-anchor-est-ta01 is not allowed. Syntax: crypto pki ta-profile <TA-NAME>.

document_title: Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series)
source_file: 8100/10_18/Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the crypto pki ta-profile command?
The syntax of the crypto pki ta-profile command is: crypto pki ta-profile <TA-NAME>

document_title: Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series)
source_file: 8100/10_18/Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the crypto pki est-profile command do?

The crypto pki est-profile command creates a certificate Enrollment over Secure Transport (EST) profile and changes to the config-est-<EST- NAME> context for the profile. Each EST profile stores information about the EST service, including EST server URL Up to 16 profiles can be created. Public crypto pki est-profile 446 If the specified EST profile exists, this command changes to the config-est-<EST-NAME> context for the profile. The no form of this command deletes the specified EST profile. It also deletes the TA profiles whose CA certificates were downloaded from the corresponding EST server, and the leaf certificates that were enrolled using this EST profile. NOTE The deletion of the related TA profiles and enrolled certificates is permanent. Syntax: crypto pki est-profile <EST-NAME>.

The syntax of the crypto pki est-profile command is: crypto pki est-profile <EST-NAME>

document_title: Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series)

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the apply macsec policy command do?

The apply macsec policy command within the selected interface context, applies the specified MAC sec policy to the selected port. When a MAC sec policy is applied to a port, MAC sec is enabled on the port and all data traffic is blocked on the port until a secure channel is successfully established. NOTE A MAC sec policy can be applied to a physical interface port that is not part of any LAG ports or to a lag port. It can also be applied to an interface that is configured as an MCLAG, VSX keep-alive, or VSX inter-switch-link. Public MAC sec commands 493 If a MAC sec policy is already applied to the selected port, this command replaces the existing policy application. NOTE For MAC sec to work, an MKA policy must also be configured and applied to the same ports. Syntax: apply macsec policy <MACSEC-POLICY-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the apply mka policy command do?

The apply mka policy command within the selected interface context, applies the specified MKA policy to the selected port. To start the MKA protocol on the port, a MAC sec policy must also be applied to the port. NOTE An MKA policy can be applied to a physical interface port that is not part of any LAG ports or to a lag port. It can also be applied to an interface that is configured as an MCLAG, VSX keep-alive, or VSX inter-switch-link. If an MKA policy is already applied to the selected port, this command replaces the existing policy application. The no form of this command dissociates the specified policy from the port. Specifies the MKA policy name. Range: 1 to 32 alphanumeric ch aracters including only the three special characters "." (period), " -" (hyphen), and "_" (underscore). <MKA-POLICY-NAME>. Syntax: apply mka policy <MKA-POLICY-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear mka statistics command do?

The clear mka statistics command clears MKA statistics on all MAC sec-enabled interfaces or on a specific interface or interface range. MKA statistics are cleared for the entire switch rather than just in the current user session. interface <IF-RANGE> Specifies one or more interfaces (ports) for which MKA statisti cs information is to be cleared. Configures the MAC sec policy to use data delay protection. Data delay protection allows MKA participants to ensure that the data frames protected by MAC sec are not delayed by more than 2 seconds. Enabling data delay protection necessitates transmission of MKPDUs at a frequency of 0.5 second to meet a maximum data delay of 2 seconds while minimizing connectivity interruption due to the possibility of lost or delayed MKPDUs. Syntax: clear mka statistics [interface <IF-RANGE>].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command configures the RADIUS authentication method for MAC authentication. Following are the MAC authentication methods supported: • CHAP Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-metho... 578 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h a u t h - m e t h o... • PAP NOTE The PEAP-MSCHAPv2 method of authentication is not supported. The no form of the command resets the authentication method to the default, chap. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command configures the period during which the switch does not try to authenticate a rejected client. The no form of the command resets the quiet period to the default, 60 seconds. Specifies the quiet period (in seconds). Default: 60. Range: 0 to 65535. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. NOTE When the RADIUS server group for MAC authentication is updated on a port, any existing clients on the port that were authenticated using the previous globally configured group will associate with the new group for the port during the next re-authentication cycle. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command enables periodic MAC reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Configures the period after which MAC authenticated clients must be reauthenticated on the port. You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the MAC reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the MAC reauthentication period (in seconds). Default: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth.

The aaa authentication port-access client-limit m command configures the data client limit on the multidomain enabled interface. By default, the data client limit on a multidomain enabled interface is 1, and the maximum number of data clients supported on a multidomain enabled port is 5. Public aaa authentication port-access client-limit multi-... 606 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s c l i e n t - l i m i t m u l t i -... Specifies the maximum data client limit on the multidomain ena bled interface. Range: 1 to 5. <DATA-CLIENT-LIMIT>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access client-limit multi-domain <DATA-CLIENT-LIMIT>.

The role name is not displayed for clients that do not use local, downloaded, or RADIUS overridden role. Specifies all interfaces. Specifies the interface name. Specifies the client MAC address. messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear dot1x supplicant statistics command do?

The clear dot1x supplicant statistics command clears the 802.1X supplicant statistics associated with the interface. If no interface is specified, the statistics are cleared for all 802.1X supplicant-enabled interfaces. Public clear dot1x supplicant statistics 745 Specifies the range of VLAN interfaces for which the supplicant statistics are cleared. <IFRANGE> Configures the time period (in seconds) to wait for a potential 802.1X authenticator on the other end before considering the link to be non-802.1X-capable and opening the interface on the data-plane. On a timeout, the switch will not use the authentication result to determine the forwarding behavior of the interface until a link flap. If not set, the switch will wait for the 802.1X authentication cycle to complete before determining the forwarding state of the interface. Syntax: clear dot1x supplicant statistics [interface <IFRANGE>].

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the aaa radius-attribute group command do?

The aaa radius-attribute group command configures an existing RADIUS server group for which the configured RADIUS attributes will be included in request packets. Enters the config-radius-attr context. The no form of this command unconfigures the RADIUS server group for the configured RADIUS attributes. NOTE Nas-id and tunnel-private-group-id attributes only apply to port access requests. Nas-ip-addr attributes only apply to management user requests. Specifies an existing RADIUS server group name. <GROUP-NAME>. Syntax: aaa radius-attribute group <GROUP-NAME>.

Selects the name of the RADIUS proxy profile. Maximum of 32 c haracters. <PROFILE-NAME> Configures the RADIUS dynamic authorization proxy client for the client group. messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the ip (RADIUS proxy profile) command do?

The ip (RADIUS proxy profile) command configures the RADIUS proxy IP address for the profile. This is a required parameter for the functionality of the proxy feature. The no form of this removes the RADIUS IP address from the profile. Public ip (RADIUS proxy profile) 821 RADIUS proxy IP address. <IPv4|IPv6> Configures the Network Access Server identifier value. The NAS ID replaces the existing NAS ID value from the RADIUS packet while sending the packet to the home server. The no form of this removes the Network Access Server identifier value. ASCII string. Minimum length is 1 character and maximum lengt h is 253 characters. <WORD>. Syntax: ip <IPv4|IPv6>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the radius-proxy client-group command do?

The radius-proxy client-group command configures the RADIUS proxy client group. A maximum of 32 RADIUS proxy client groups can be configured on the device. The no form of this command removes the configured client group. Public radius-proxy client-group 830 Specifies the name of the RADIUS proxy client group. Maximum of 32 characters. <GROUP-NAME>. A maximum of 16 RADIUS dynamic authorization proxy client groups can be configured on the device. The no form of this command removes the client group. messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the radius-proxy enable command do?

The radius-proxy enable command enables RADIUS proxy at the global level. RADIUS proxy is disabled by default. This command is required for the RADIUS proxy functionality to work on the switch. The no form of this command disables the RADIUS proxy functionality. Syntax: radius-proxy enable.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the radius-proxy profile command do?

The radius-proxy profile command configures the RADIUS proxy profile. The profile has all of the client and the server details to facilitate the proxy functionality. The profule must be enabled at the current context level after configuring all of the required parameters along with enabling the RADIUS proxy globally. A maximum of 32 RADIUS profiles can be configured on the device. The no form of this command removes the RADIUS proxy profile. Selects the name of the RADIUS proxy profile. Maximum of 32 c haracters. <PROFILE-NAME>. Syntax: radius-proxy profile <PROFILE-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show capacities radius-gleaned-client-entries command do?

The show capacities radius-gleaned-client-entries command displays the maximum number of Port-Access clients that can be gleaned by RADIUS proxy on a device. The status version of the command displays the count of the Port-Access clients currently gleaned by RADIUS proxy on the device. Syntax: show capacities radius-gleaned-client-entries.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the show running-config (radius-proxy) command do?

The show running-config (radius-proxy) command displays the current running configuration. Configures the RADIUS server timeout interval in seconds. The proxy waits for this time interval to get a response from the home server. This value should be equal to or greater than the NAS RADIUS timeout value. The no form of this command resets the RADIUS server timeout interval to its default value of 5 seconds. RADIUS server timeout interval in seconds. Default: 5 seconds. <SECONDS>. Syntax: show running-config.

The vrf (RADIUS dynamic authorization proxy profi command configures the VRF to use in the profile. The no form of this command restores theVRF to the default VRF in the profile. Specifies the VRF name. Default: default. <VRF-NAME>. Syntax: vrf <VRF-NAME>.

The show port-access port-security interface port command shows port security statistics for the ports in a switch. The output can be filtered by interface. Selects all interfaces. Public show port-access port-security interface port-stat... 866 s h o w p o r t - a c c e s s p o r t - s e c u r i t y i n t e r f a c e p o r t - s t a t... Specifies the interface name. <IF-NAME>. Syntax: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>} port-statistics.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the apply gbp role-access-list command do?

The apply gbp role-access-list command applies the GBP role access list at the system level. When a new IPv4 or IPv6 GBP ACL is applied, the previously applied IPv4 or IPv6 GBP ACL is replaced with the new ACL. This command is supported only on profile: Core-Spine. The no form of this command removes the applied GBP role access list. Specifies the GBP role access list type, ip for IPv4, or IPv6 for I Pv6. ip|IPv6 <GBP_ACL_NAME>. Syntax: [no] apply gbp role-access-list {ip|IPv6} <GBP_ACL_NAME>.

source_file: 8100/10_18/Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the class gbp-ip command do?

The class gbp-ip command creates, deletes, or modifies an IPv4 Group-Based Policy (GBP) class to match specified protocol packets. A class consists of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each class can classify traffic based on IPv4 protocol header information. The no keyword deletes either a class or an individual class entry. Specifies the class name. <CLASS-NAME> Public class gbp-ip 903 Specifies the class entry sequence number. Range: 1 to 429496 7295. <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> {match | ignore} <SRC-ROLE-NAME> <DST-ROLE-NAME> <PORT-NUMBER> <MIN-PORT> <MAX-PORT> <ICMP-TYPE-VALUE> <ICMP-CODE-VALUE> Creates a rule to ignore or match specified IPv4 packets. Specifies the source role name. Specifies the destination role name. Specifies the layer 4 port number. Range: 0 to 65535. Syntax: [no] class gbp-ip <CLASS-NAME>.

document_title: Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series)
source_file: 8100/10_18/Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what is the syntax of the class gbp-ip command?
The syntax of the class gbp-ip command is: [no] class gbp-ip <CLASS-NAME>

source_file: 8100/10_18/Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the class gbp-IPv6 command do?

The class gbp-IPv6 command creates, deletes, or modifies an IPv6 Group-Based Policy (GBP) class to match specified protocol packets.. A class consists of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each class can classify traffic based on IPv6 protocol header information. The no keyword deletes either a class or an individual class entry. Specifies the class name. <CLASS-NAME> <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> {match | ignore} <SRC-ROLE-NAME> <DST-ROLE-NAME> <PORT-NUMBER> <MIN-PORT> Specifies the class entry sequence number. Range: 1 to 429496 7295. Creates a rule to ignore or match specified IPv6 packets. Specifies the source role name. Specifies the destination role name. Specifies the layer 4 port number. Range: 0 to 65535. Specifies the start port number in the range. Range: 0 to 65535. Syntax: class gbp-IPv6 <CLASS-NAME>.

The syntax of the class gbp-IPv6 command is: class gbp-IPv6 <CLASS-NAME>

source_file: 8100/10_18/Security Guide (8100, 8360 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the class gbp-mac command do?

The class gbp-mac command creates, deletes, or modifies class to match specified protocol packets. A class consists of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each class can classify traffic based on MAC information. The no keyword deletes either a class or an individual class entry. Specifies the class name. <CLASS-NAME> <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> {match | ignore} <SRC-ROLE-NAME> <DST-ROLE-NAME> <NUMERIC-ETHERTYPE> Specifies the class entry sequence number. Range: 1 to 429496 7295. Creates a rule to ignore or match specified packets. Specifies the source role name. Specifies the destination role name. Specifies the Ether Type number. Range: 0x600 to 0xffff. Public class gbp-mac 912. Syntax: class gbp-mac <CLASS-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.18, what does the clear port-access gbp hitcounts command do?

The clear port-access gbp hitcounts command clears the statistics of the group based policy applied on the client. Public clear port-access gbp hitcounts 915 Specifies the CDP TLV number. Supported values are 1 to 6, 10, and 11. <TLV-NUMBER>. Syntax: clear port-access gbp [<POLICY-NAME>] hitcounts {client}.
